case_title: HELEN LAU VS. NELIA BEDIA NACOR ET AL

docket_entries.description: DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, PREVIOUSLY SUBMITTED ON FEB-15-2022, THE COURT NOW GRANTS IN PART AND DENIES IN PART DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS. Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Thomas J Murray In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. VADIM NEBUCHIN, Esq. of Callaway & Wolf (Tel: 415-541-0300) for plaintiff;
Atty. ANA M. DAVILA, Esq. of Kern Segal & Murray (Tel: 415-474-1900) for defendants Nelia Nacor and Crisanto Canosa.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS NELIA NACOR and CRISANTO CANOSA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for parties (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
